---
title: "Exploratory: Instagram Comments Text Analysis"
description: "What types of comments do young people write, and what is the sentiment of the comments?" 

format:
  html:
    theme: paper
    shift-heading-level-by: 1
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
    toc-fold: false
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-title: "Contents"

# Portability settings
embed-resources: true

# Execution options 
execute:
  warning: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
# set error messages to English
Sys.setenv(LANG = "en")

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.align = "center", 
  fig.retina = 3,
  fig.width = 6, 
  fig.height = (6 * 0.618),
  out.width = "90%", 
  collapse = FALSE
)

options(
  digits = 4,
  width = 300,
  dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#| include: false
#| warning: true
#| message: true
if (!require("groundhog", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("groundhog")
}
library("groundhog")

# use groundhog to install and load packages
pkgs <- c("here",         # System path management
          "tidyverse",    # ggplot, dplyr, and friends
          "tinytable",    # Minimalist tables
          "modelsummary", # Data and model summaries with tables and plots
          "marginaleffects", # Model-based predictions, comparisons, and more marginalia
          "ellmer",       # Interact with LLM APIs in R
          "tidyprompt",   # Nice way to prompt LLMs 
          "stringr",      # Additional string operations
          "tidytext",     # Text Mining using 'dplyr', 'ggplot2', and Other Tidy Tools
          "ggwordcloud",  # Wordclouds in ggplot2
          "ggdist",       # Additional plot elements
          "ggrain",       # For fancy raincloud plots
          "GGally",       # ggpairs and other ggplot2 friends
          "ggsignif",     # Add significance to figures
          "patchwork",    # Combine multiple ggplots
          "showtext"      # Additional fonts for plots etc
          )

groundhog.library(pkgs, "2026-01-01")
```

```{r}
#| include: false

showtext_auto()
showtext_opts(dpi = 600)

font_add_google("Public Sans", "Public Sans")
font_add_google("Public Sans", "Public Sans Light", regular.wt = 300)
font_add_google("Public Sans", "Public Sans Semibold", regular.wt = 600)

# Custom ggplot theme
theme_nice <- function() {
  # General text family and base size
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Public Sans", base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      # Remove minor grid lines for cleaner look
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      # Customize major grid lines color
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      # Plot title: bold and custom relative size
      plot.title = element_text(family = "Public Sans", face = "bold", size = rel(1.1), margin = margin(b = 10)),
      # Subtitle and caption: lighter weight 
      plot.subtitle = element_text(family = "Public Sans Light", face = "plain"),
      plot.caption = element_text(family = "Public Sans Light", face = "plain"),
      # Axis text: Relative size
      axis.text = element_text(size = rel(0.9)),
      # Axis titles: Semibold and custom relative size
      axis.title = element_text(family = "Public Sans Semibold", size = rel(0.9)),
      axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title.y = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      # Axis lines
      axis.line = element_line(color = "grey25", linewidth = 0.8),
      # Facet labels: bold text on grey background
      strip.text = element_text(
        family = "Public Sans Semibold", face = "plain",
        size = rel(1),
        hjust = 0.5
      ),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = "grey25", linewidth = 0.8), 
      # Legend styling: compact and top-positioned
      legend.title = element_text(family = "Public Sans Semibold", size = rel(0.8)),
      legend.text = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.justification = "center",
      legend.title.position = "left",
      legend.margin = margin(l = 0, t = 0)
    )
}


theme_set(theme_nice())
update_geom_defaults("text", list(family = "Public Sans"))
update_geom_defaults("label", list(family = "Public Sans"))
```

```{r}
#| include: false
data_dir <- here("01_data")
data_analysis_dir <- here("01_data", "analysis")
table_dir <- here("02_output", "tables", "exploratory")
llm_dir <- here("02_output", "LLM")
figure_dir <- here("02_output", "figures", "exploratory")

dir.create(data_analysis_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = TRUE)
dir.create(table_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = TRUE)
dir.create(figure_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = TRUE)
dir.create(llm_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = TRUE)
```

```{r}
load(here(data_dir, "clean", "df_wide.RData"))
load(here(data_dir, "clean", "df_long.RData"))
```

## Quick Look at Data

```{r}
df_wide |> 
  select(exp_condition, exp_comment) |> 
  head(10) |> 
  tt()
```

## Wordclouds

#### Prepare the Data

```{r}
# Tokenize comments
comment_words_raw <- df_wide |>
  select(id, exp_condition, exp_comment) |>
  filter(!is.na(exp_comment), exp_comment != "") |>
  unnest_tokens(word, exp_comment)

# Expanded custom stop words
custom_stop <- tibble(word = c(
  # Meta-words about the task
  "comment", "post", "instagram", "share", "write",
  
  # Meta-responses (people saying they wouldn't comment)
  "no", "not", "nothing", "would", "wouldn't", "wouldn’t", "idk",
  
  # Contractions 
  "it's", "i'm", "don't", "dont", "can't", "that's", "i've", "isn't",
  "didn't", "doesn't", "haven't", "won't", "aren't", "wasn't",
  "you're", "they're", "we're", "i'll", "there's", "here's",
  "it’s", "i’m", "don’t", "can’t", "that’s", "i’ve", "isn’t",
  "didn’t", "doesn’t", "haven’t", "won’t", "aren’t", "wasn’t", "i’d",  
  "you’re", "they’re", "we’re", "i’ll", "there’s", "here’s", "let’s" 
))

# Clean and prepare words for wordcloud
comments_text <- comment_words_raw |>
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") |>
  anti_join(custom_stop, by = "word") |>
  filter(
    str_length(word) >= 3,
    !str_detect(word, "^\\d+$"),
    !str_detect(word, "[''']")
  ) |>
  count(exp_condition, word, sort = TRUE) |>
  group_by(exp_condition) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 75) |>
  ungroup()

# Check final counts
comments_text |> 
  count(exp_condition)

# Preview top words per condition
comments_text |>
  group_by(exp_condition) |>
  slice_head(n = 15) |> 
  tt()
```

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-dpi: 600
#| fig-showtext: TRUE

comments_text |>  
  ggplot(aes(label = word, size = n, color = n)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud_area(
    rm_outside = TRUE,
    eccentricity = 0.65
  ) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 20) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colours = c("#313695", "#4575b4", "#74add1", "#a50026", "#d73027", "#f46d43")) +
  facet_wrap(~exp_condition, labeller = as_labeller(c(
    anxiety = "Anxiety",
    hope = "Hope", 
    control = "Control"))
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 18)
  )

ggsave(here(figure_dir, "comments_wordcloud.svg"),
       height = 4,
       width = 11,
       dpi = 600)
```

## Sentiment Analysis

### Interact with LLM

::: {.callout-important}
## Don't forget to remove own API code before sharing the code. 
:::

```{r}
Sys.setenv(ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = "PASTE YOUR API CODE")
```

```{r}
chat_sentiment <- chat_anthropic(
  system_prompt = "You are a sentiment analyst. Rate each comment objectively using the exact scale provided.",
  model = "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
  echo = "none",
  api_args = list(
    temperature = 0,      # Deterministic output
    max_tokens = 10       # We only need 1-2 tokens (a number or "NA")
  )
)
```

```{r}
# Create tidyprompt provider
provider_sentiment <- llm_provider_ellmer(chat_sentiment)
```

```{r}
# Define the prompt
prompt_sentiment <- 'How negative or positive is this comment on a 1 to 7 scale? Answer only with a number, with 1 being "very negative", 4 being "neutral", and 7 being "very positive." If the comment is nonsensical or a non-response (e.g., "no", "none", "would not comment"), return 99. Here is the comment:'

print(prompt_sentiment)
```

##### Quick test

```{r}
test_df <- tibble(
  id = 1:5,
  exp_comment = c(
    "I am terrified and so sad :(",
    "statistics show inflation has changed recently",
    "there is always so much hope out there in the world!",
    "glagalmgadn",
    "no"
  )
)

# Run sentiment analysis
# test_df <- test_df |>
#   mutate(
#     sentiment = map_int(exp_comment, function(comment) {
#       paste0(prompt_sentiment, "\n\n", comment) |>
#         answer_as_integer(min = 1, max = 99) |>  # Force integer in valid range
#         send_prompt(provider_sentiment, verbose = FALSE)
#     }, .progress = TRUE)
#   )
```

```{r}
# test_df |> tt()
```

### Run the LLM Analysis

```{r}
comments_df <- df_wide |>
  select(id, exp_comment)

nrow(comments_df)
```

```{r}
# comments_sentiment <- comments_df |>
#   mutate(
#     sentiment_score = map_int(exp_comment, function(comment) {
#       paste0(prompt_sentiment, "\n\n", comment) |>
#         answer_as_integer(min = 1, max = 99) |>  # Force integer in valid range
#         send_prompt(provider_sentiment, verbose = FALSE)
#     }, .progress = TRUE)
#   )
# 
# save(comments_sentiment, file = here(llm_dir, "comments_sentiment.RData"))
# write_csv(comments_sentiment, file = here(llm_dir, "comments_sentiment.csv"))
```

```{r}
# If code above already ran, use this to load the dataframe and save some energy and money
load(here(llm_dir, "comments_sentiment.RData"))
```

#### Visualise the Findings

```{r}
comments_sentiment <- comments_sentiment |>
  mutate(
    sentiment = case_when(
      sentiment_score == 99 ~ NA_real_,
      sentiment_score >= 1 & sentiment_score <= 7 ~ as.numeric(sentiment_score),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_  # Catch any unexpected values
    )
  )
```

```{r}
df_wide <- df_wide |>
  left_join(
    comments_sentiment |> select(id, exp_comment_sentiment = sentiment),
    by = "id"
  )
```

```{r}
df_wide |> 
  select(Treatment = exp_condition,
         Sentiment = exp_comment_sentiment) |> 
  datasummary_balance(~Treatment, 
                      data = _)
```

```{r}
df_wide |> 
  select(Sentiment = exp_comment_sentiment,
         "Anxiety - Hope" = anxiety_hope_dim,
         Anxious = exp_check_anxious,
         Angry = exp_check_angry,
         Hopeful = exp_check_hopeful,
         Happy = exp_check_happy,
         Calm = exp_check_calm) |> 
  datasummary_correlation(data = _)
```

```{r}
scatter_function <- function(data, mapping, ...) {
  ggplot(data = data, mapping = mapping) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60") +
    geom_point(color = "#c994c7", alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = T, linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.5)
}
```

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-height: 8.5
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-dpi: 600
#| fig-showtext: TRUE
showtext_opts(dpi = 600)
df_wide |> 
  mutate(anxiety_hope_dim = anxiety_hope_dim*100) |> 
  select(Sentiment = exp_comment_sentiment,
         "A-H Dim " = anxiety_hope_dim,
         Anxious = exp_check_anxious,
         Angry = exp_check_angry,
         Hopeful = exp_check_hopeful,
         Happy = exp_check_happy,
         Calm = exp_check_calm)|> 
  ggpairs(
    data = _,
    # Lower Triangle: Scatterplot
    lower = list(continuous = scatter_function),
    # Upper Triangle: Correlations 
    upper = list(continuous = wrap("cor", stars = TRUE, size = 5, color = "black", family = "Public Sans")),
    # Diagonal: Density 
    diag = list(continuous = wrap("densityDiag", fill = "#c994c7", color = "black"))
  ) +
  theme_nice() 

ggsave(here(figure_dir, "sent_manip_corrs.png"),
       height = 8.5,
       width = 12,
       dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-showtext: TRUE
df_wide |> 
  filter(!is.na(exp_comment_sentiment)) |> 
  mutate(exp_condition = factor(exp_condition, levels = c("anxiety", "hope", "control"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = exp_condition, y = exp_comment_sentiment, fill = exp_condition, color = exp_condition)) +
  geom_rain(
    rain.side = "r",  
    seed = 42,
    point.args = list(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8),  
    boxplot.args = list(
      outlier.shape = NA,
      alpha = 0.7,
      color = "black"  
    ),
    violin.args = list(alpha = 0.7)
  ) +
  geom_signif(
    aes(x = exp_condition, y = exp_comment_sentiment),  
    comparisons = list(
      c("anxiety", "hope"), 
      c("anxiety", "control"), 
      c("hope", "control")
    ),
    map_signif_level = TRUE,
    size = 0.8,
    textsize = 4,
    family = "Public Sans",
    color = "black",
    tip_length = 0,
    step_increase = 0.12,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2", direction = -1) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", direction = -1) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Sentiment",
    title = "Sentiment by Experimental Condition"
  ) +
  theme_nice() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") 

ggsave(here(figure_dir, "sent_raincloud.svg"),
       height = 4,
       width = 6,
       dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
sent1 <- lm(exp_comment_sentiment ~ exp_condition, data = df_wide)

sent1_comp <- avg_comparisons(sent1, 
                              variables = list(exp_condition = "pairwise")) |> 
  as_tibble() |> 
  select(Contrast = contrast,
         Estimate = estimate,
         SE = std.error,
         `p` = p.value,
         "95% CI Lower" = conf.low,
         "95% CI Upper" = conf.high
         ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), \(x) round(x, 3)))

sent1_comp |> tt()
```

## Discrete Emotion Analysis

```{r}
# Create new chat object for emotion coding
chat_emotion <- chat_anthropic(
  system_prompt = "You are an emotion analyst. Classify each comment objectively using the coding provided.",
  model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  echo = "none",
  api_args = list(
    temperature = 0,
    max_tokens = 10
  )
)

# Create provider
provider_emotion <- llm_provider_ellmer(chat_emotion)

# Define emotion prompt
prompt_emotion <- 'Which of these 5 emotions – angry, anxious, hopeful, happy, calm – best represents the general emotion expressed in the following comment? Answer only with a number: 1 if angry, 2 if anxious, 3 if hopeful, 4 if happy, 5 if calm. If the comment is nonsensical or a non-response (e.g., "no", "none", "would not comment"), return 99. Here is the comment:'

print(prompt_emotion)
```

##### Quick test

```{r}
# test_df <- test_df |>
#   mutate(
#     emotion_raw = map_int(exp_comment, function(comment) {
#       paste0(prompt_emotion, "\n\n", comment) |>
#         answer_as_integer(min = 1, max = 99) |>
#         send_prompt(provider_emotion, verbose = FALSE)
#     }, .progress = TRUE)
#   )
```

```{r}
# Recode to factor with labels
# test_df <- test_df |>
#   mutate(
#     emotion = case_when(
#       emotion_raw == 1 ~ "angry",
#       emotion_raw == 2 ~ "anxious",
#       emotion_raw == 3 ~ "hopeful",
#       emotion_raw == 4 ~ "happy",
#       emotion_raw == 5 ~ "calm",
#       emotion_raw == 99 ~ NA_character_,
#       TRUE ~ NA_character_
#     ),
#     emotion = factor(emotion, levels = c("angry", "anxious", "hopeful", "happy", "calm"))
#   )
# 
# test_df |>
#   select(exp_comment, emotion_raw, emotion) |> 
#   tt()
```

### Run the LLM Analysis

```{r}
# # Run emotion coding on data
# comments_emotion <- comments_df |>
#   mutate(
#     emotion_raw = map_int(exp_comment, function(comment) {
#       paste0(prompt_emotion, "\n\n", comment) |>
#         answer_as_integer(min = 1, max = 99) |>
#         send_prompt(provider_emotion, verbose = FALSE)
#     }, .progress = TRUE)
#   )
# 
# # Save raw results
# save(comments_emotion, file = here(llm_dir, "comments_emotion.RData"))
# write_csv(comments_emotion, file = here(llm_dir, "comments_emotion.csv"))
```

```{r}
# If code above already ran, use this to load the dataframe and save some energy and money
load(here(llm_dir, "comments_emotion.RData"))
```

```{r}
# Recode to labeled factor
comments_emotion <- comments_emotion |>
  mutate(
    emotion = case_when(
      emotion_raw == 1 ~ "angry",
      emotion_raw == 2 ~ "anxious",
      emotion_raw == 3 ~ "hopeful",
      emotion_raw == 4 ~ "happy",
      emotion_raw == 5 ~ "calm",
      emotion_raw == 99 ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    emotion = factor(emotion, levels = c("angry", "anxious", "hopeful", "happy", "calm"))
  )

comments_emotion |>
  count(emotion_raw, emotion) |> tt()
```

```{r}
df_wide <- df_wide |>
  left_join(
    comments_emotion |> select(id, exp_comment_emotion = emotion),
    by = "id"
  )
```

#### Visualise the Findings

```{r}
emotion_crosstab <- df_wide |> 
  select(Treatment = exp_condition,
         Emotion = exp_comment_emotion) |> 
  datasummary_crosstab(Emotion~Treatment, 
                       data = _)

emotion_crosstab 

emotion_crosstab |> 
  save_tt(here(table_dir, "emotion_crosstab.docx"), overwrite = T)
```

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-dpi: 600
#| fig-showtext: TRUE
# Define emotion colors 
emotion_colors <- c(
  "angry" = "#e07a7a",      
  "anxious" = "#f4a582",  
  "hopeful" = "#a8ddb5",    
  "happy" = "#ffd97d",     
  "calm" = "#7eb3d4"        
)

# Prepare the data
emotion_plot_data <- df_wide |>
  filter(!is.na(exp_comment_emotion)) |>
  count(exp_condition, exp_comment_emotion) |>
  group_by(exp_condition) |>
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    pct = n / total * 100,
    label = if_else(pct >= 1, sprintf("%.1f%%", pct), "") 
  ) |>
  ungroup()

emotion_plot_data |> ggplot(
  aes(x = exp_condition, y = pct, fill = exp_comment_emotion)
  ) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.7, color = "grey20") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "plain",
    family = "Public Sans Semibold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = emotion_colors,
    name = "Emotion",
    labels = c("Angry", "Anxious", "Hopeful", "Happy", "Calm")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c("Control", "Anxiety", "Hope")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Experimental Condition",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank()) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse=T))

ggsave(here(figure_dir, "discrete_emotion_dist.svg"),
       height = 5,
       width = 8,
       dpi = 600)
```

## Save the Data with Sentiment & Emotion Codings

### Wide data

```{r}
df_wide <- df_wide |> 
  select(id:exp_comment, 
         exp_comment_sentiment, exp_comment_emotion,
         exp_reaction:post_attention1)
```

```{r}
save(df_wide, file = here(data_analysis_dir, "df_wide.RData"))
write_csv(df_wide, file = here(data_analysis_dir, "df_wide.csv"))
```

### Long data

```{r}
df_long <- df_long |> 
  left_join(df_wide |> select(id, exp_comment_sentiment, exp_comment_emotion), by = "id") |> 
  select(id:exp_comment,
         exp_comment_sentiment, exp_comment_emotion,
         exp_reaction:post_attention1)
```

```{r}
save(df_long, file = here(data_analysis_dir, "df_long.RData"))
write_csv(df_long, file = here(data_analysis_dir, "df_long.csv"))
```
